item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you go to page 4 of SCP-1584's discussion page, you'll find that the article's title is really just a website. The problem there was that a domain bearing that same website's name (as well as the TLD) popped up after the article.

tl;dr: Voct will most likely tell you to change the URL because of legal reasons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fair enough. I guess I didn't realize how disproportionately large the headers were in comparison. I think this article could use a quick polish after the contest to fix a lot of the issues that neither my partner nor the beta readers caught.

Also, I feel like you should change or hide the title's domain in a blackbox. Just in case something like this happens again.

I don't know what this means but I will fix it as soon as I have access to a computer (as long as it doesn't also violate contest rules).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted due to the headers being too big that it distracts the whole thing and taking a rather significant portion of the article. There's also too many crosslinks bunched together so closely that it drags the whole flow down.

Also, I feel like you should change or hide the title's domain in a blackbox. Just in case something like this happens again.

item.title: Grand Old Time
item.content:encoded: It was a delight working with you Tech, and I’m proud of what we’ve made here. I hope we can collab again in the future.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Noting here for the record that Footnote Three was added after posting. This does not constitute an allowed edit under Collaboration Contest rules. While the footnote may be re-added after the contest ends, it has been removed by Edna Granbo at my order for the duration of the contest.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded: Written with the help of my collaborator Popsioak for Collabcon 2019. Thanks to Clem from SCP Declassified Discord for crit.
